84:   scope :visible, (lambda do |*args|
85:     joins(:project).
86:     where(Issue.visible_condition(args.shift || User.current, *args))
87:   end)

95:   scope :recently_updated, lambda {order(:updated_on => :desc)}

99:   end)
100:   scope :fixed_version, (lambda do |versions|
101:     ids = [versions].flatten.compact.map {|v| v.is_a?(Version) ? v.id : v}
102:     ids.any? ? where(:fixed_version_id => ids) : none
103:   end)
104:   scope :assigned_to, (lambda do |arg|
105:     arg = Array(arg).uniq
106:     ids = arg.map {|p| p.is_a?(Principal) ? p.id : p}
107:     ids += arg.grep(User).map(&:group_ids).flatten.uniq
108:     ids.compact!
109:     ids.any? ? where(:assigned_to_id => ids) : none
110:   end)
111:   scope :like, (lambda do |q|
112:     if q.present?
113:       where(*::Query.tokenized_like_conditions("#{table_name}.subject", q))
114:     end
115:   end)

136:   # Returns a SQL conditions string used to find all issues visible by the specified user
137:   def self.visible_condition(user, options={})
138:     Project.allowed_to_condition(user, :view_issues, options) do |role, user|
139:       sql =
140:         if user.id && user.logged?
141:           case role.issues_visibility
142:           when 'all'
143:             '1=1'
144:           when 'default'
145:             user_ids = [user.id] + user.groups.pluck(:id).compact
146:             "(#{table_name}.is_private = #{connection.quoted_false} " \
147:               "OR #{table_name}.author_id = #{user.id} " \
148:               "OR #{table_name}.assigned_to_id IN (#{user_ids.join(',')}))"
149:           when 'own'
150:             user_ids = [user.id] + user.groups.pluck(:id).compact
151:             "(#{table_name}.author_id = #{user.id} OR " \
152:               "#{table_name}.assigned_to_id IN (#{user_ids.join(',')}))"
153:           else
154:             '1=0'
155:           end
156:         else
157:           "(#{table_name}.is_private = #{connection.quoted_false})"
158:         end
159:       unless role.permissions_all_trackers?(:view_issues)
160:         tracker_ids = role.permissions_tracker_ids(:view_issues)
161:         if tracker_ids.any?
162:           sql = "(#{sql} AND #{table_name}.tracker_id IN (#{tracker_ids.join(',')}))"
163:         else
164:           sql = '1=0'
165:         end
166:       end
167:       sql
168:     end
169:   end

171:   # Returns true if usr or current user is allowed to view the issue
172:   def visible?(usr=nil)
173:     (usr || User.current).allowed_to?(:view_issues, self.project) do |role, user|
174:       visible =
175:         if user.logged?
176:           case role.issues_visibility
177:           when 'all'
178:             true
179:           when 'default'
180:             !self.is_private? || (self.author == user || user.is_or_belongs_to?(assigned_to))
181:           when 'own'
182:             self.author == user || user.is_or_belongs_to?(assigned_to)
183:           else
184:             false
185:           end
186:         else
187:           !self.is_private?
188:         end
189:       unless role.permissions_all_trackers?(:view_issues)
190:         visible &&= role.permissions_tracker_ids?(:view_issues, tracker_id)
191:       end
192:       visible
193:     end
194:   end

196:   # Returns true if user or current user is allowed to log time on the issue
197:   def time_loggable?(user=User.current)
198:     user.allowed_to?(:log_time, project) && (Setting.timelog_accept_closed_issues? || !closed?)
199:   end

218:   def attachments_editable?(user=User.current)

237:   def initialize(attributes=nil, *args)
238:     super
239:     if new_record?
240:       # set default values for new records only
241:       self.priority ||= IssuePriority.default
242:       self.watcher_user_ids = []
243:     end
244:   end

246:   def create_or_update(*args)
247:     super()
248:   ensure
249:     @status_was = nil
250:   end
251:   private :create_or_update
252: 
253:   # AR#Persistence#destroy would raise and RecordNotFound exception
254:   # if the issue was already deleted or updated (non matching lock_version).
255:   # This is a problem when bulk deleting issues or deleting a project
256:   # (because an issue may already be deleted if its parent was deleted
257:   # first).
258:   # The issue is reloaded by the nested_set before being deleted so
259:   # the lock_version condition should not be an issue but we handle it.
260:   def destroy
261:     super
262:   rescue ActiveRecord::StaleObjectError, ActiveRecord::RecordNotFound
263:     # Stale or already deleted
264:     begin
265:       reload
266:     rescue ActiveRecord::RecordNotFound
267:       # The issue was actually already deleted
268:       @destroyed = true
269:       return freeze
270:     end
271:     # The issue was stale, retry to destroy
272:     super
273:   end
274: 
275:   alias :base_reload :reload
276:   def reload(*)
277:     @workflow_rule_by_attribute = nil
278:     @assignable_versions = nil
279:     @relations = nil
280:     @spent_hours = nil
281:     @total_spent_hours = nil
282:     @total_estimated_hours = nil
283:     @last_updated_by = nil
284:     @last_notes = nil
285:     base_reload(*)
286:   end

288:   # Overrides Redmine::Acts::Customizable::InstanceMethods#available_custom_fields
289:   def available_custom_fields
290:     (project && tracker) ? (project.all_issue_custom_fields & tracker.custom_fields) : []
291:   end

293:   def visible_custom_field_values(user=nil)
294:     user_real = user || User.current
295:     custom_field_values.select do |value|
296:       value.custom_field.visible_by?(project, user_real)

303:   end

305:   # Copies attributes from another issue, arg can be an id or an Issue
306:   def copy_from(arg, options={})
307:     issue = arg.is_a?(Issue) ? arg : Issue.visible.find(arg)
308:     self.attributes =
309:       issue.attributes.dup.except(
310:         "id", "root_id", "parent_id", "lft", "rgt",
311:         "created_on", "updated_on", "status_id", "closed_on"
312:       )
313:     self.custom_field_values =
314:       issue.custom_field_values.to_h do |v|
315:         [v.custom_field_id, v.value]
316:       end
317:     if options[:keep_status]
318:       self.status = issue.status
319:     end
320:     self.author = User.current
321:     unless options[:attachments] == false
322:       self.attachments = issue.attachments.map do |attachement|
323:         attachement.copy(:container => self)
324:       end
325:     end
326: 
327:     unless options[:watchers] == false
328:       self.watcher_user_ids = issue.visible_watcher_users.select{|u| u.status == User::STATUS_ACTIVE}.map(&:id)
329:     end
330:     @copied_from = issue
331:     @copy_options = options
332:     self
333:   end
334: 
335:   # Returns an unsaved copy of the issue
336:   def copy(attributes=nil, copy_options={})
337:     copy = self.class.new.copy_from(self, copy_options)
338:     copy.attributes = attributes if attributes
339:     copy
340:   end

342:   # Returns true if the issue is a copy
343:   def copy?
344:     @copied_from.present?
345:   end

350:     end

355:   def status=(status)
356:     if status != self.status
357:       @workflow_rule_by_attribute = nil
358:     end
359:     association(:status).writer(status)

377:   def tracker_id=(tracker_id)
378:     if tracker_id.to_s != self.tracker_id.to_s
379:       self.tracker = (tracker_id.present? ? Tracker.find_by_id(tracker_id) : nil)
380:     end
381:     self.tracker_id
382:   end
383: 
384:   # Sets the tracker.
385:   # This will set the status to the default status of the new tracker if:
386:   # * the status was the default for the previous tracker
387:   # * or if the status was not part of the new tracker statuses
388:   # * or the status was nil
389:   def tracker=(tracker)
390:     tracker_was = self.tracker
391:     association(:tracker).writer(tracker)
392:     if tracker != tracker_was
393:       if status == tracker_was.try(:default_status)
394:         self.status = nil
395:       elsif status && tracker && !tracker.issue_status_ids.include?(status.id)
396:         self.status = nil
397:       end
398:       reassign_custom_field_values
399:       @workflow_rule_by_attribute = nil
400:     end
401:     self.status ||= default_status
402:     self.tracker
403:   end
404: 
405:   def project_id=(project_id)
406:     if project_id.to_s != self.project_id.to_s
407:       self.project = (project_id.present? ? Project.find_by_id(project_id) : nil)
408:     end
409:     self.project_id
410:   end
411: 
412:   # Sets the project.
413:   # Unless keep_tracker argument is set to true, this will change the tracker
414:   # to the first tracker of the new project if the previous tracker is not part
415:   # of the new project trackers.
416:   # This will:
417:   # * clear the fixed_version is it's no longer valid for the new project.
418:   # * clear the parent issue if it's no longer valid for the new project.
419:   # * set the category to the category with the same name in the new
420:   #   project if it exists, or clear it if it doesn't.
421:   # * for new issue, set the fixed_version to the project default version
422:   #   if it's a valid fixed_version.
423:   def project=(project, keep_tracker=false)
424:     project_was = self.project
425:     association(:project).writer(project)
426:     if project != project_was
427:       @safe_attribute_names = nil
428:     end
429:     if project_was && project && project_was != project
430:       @assignable_versions = nil
431: 
432:       unless keep_tracker || project.trackers.include?(tracker)
433:         self.tracker = project.trackers.first
434:       end
435:       # Reassign to the category with same name if any
436:       if category
437:         self.category = project.issue_categories.find_by_name(category.name)
438:       end
439:       # Clear the assignee if not available in the new project for new issues (eg. copy)
440:       # For existing issue, the previous assignee is still valid, so we keep it
441:       if new_record? && assigned_to && !assignable_users.include?(assigned_to)
442:         self.assigned_to_id = nil
443:       end
444:       # Keep the fixed_version if it's still valid in the new_project
445:       if fixed_version && fixed_version.project != project && !project.shared_versions.include?(fixed_version)
446:         self.fixed_version = nil
447:       end
448:       # Clear the parent task if it's no longer valid
449:       unless valid_parent_project?
450:         self.parent_issue_id = nil
451:       end
452:       reassign_custom_field_values
453:       @workflow_rule_by_attribute = nil
454:     end
455:     # Set fixed_version to the project default version if it's valid
456:     if new_record? && fixed_version.nil? && project && project.default_version_id?
457:       if project.shared_versions.open.exists?(project.default_version_id)
458:         self.fixed_version_id = project.default_version_id
459:       end
460:     end
461:     self.project
462:   end

541:   def safe_attribute_names(user=nil)
542:     names = super
543:     names -= disabled_core_fields
544:     names -= read_only_attribute_names(user)
545:     if new_record?
546:       # Make sure that project_id can always be set for new issues
547:       names |= %w(project_id)
548:     end
549:     if dates_derived?
550:       names -= %w(start_date due_date)
551:     end
552:     if priority_derived?
553:       names -= %w(priority_id)
554:     end
555:     if done_ratio_derived?
556:       names -= %w(done_ratio)
557:     end
558:     names
559:   end

561:   # Safely sets attributes
562:   # Should be called from controllers instead of #attributes=
563:   # attr_accessible is too rough because we still want things like
564:   # Issue.new(:project => foo) to work
565:   def safe_attributes=(attrs, user=User.current)
566:     if attrs.respond_to?(:to_unsafe_hash)
567:       attrs = attrs.to_unsafe_hash
568:     end
569: 
570:     @attributes_set_by = user
571:     return unless attrs.is_a?(Hash)
572: 
573:     attrs = attrs.deep_dup
574: 
575:     # Project and Tracker must be set before since new_statuses_allowed_to depends on it.
576:     if (p = attrs.delete('project_id')) && safe_attribute?('project_id')
577:       if p.is_a?(String) && !/^\d*$/.match?(p)
578:         p_id = Project.find_by_identifier(p).try(:id)
579:       else
580:         p_id = p.to_i
581:       end
582:       if allowed_target_projects(user).where(:id => p_id).exists?
583:         self.project_id = p_id
584:       end
585: 
586:       if project_id_changed? && attrs['category_id'].present? && attrs['category_id'].to_s == category_id_was.to_s
587:         # Discard submitted category on previous project
588:         attrs.delete('category_id')
589:       end
590:     end
591: 
592:     if (t = attrs.delete('tracker_id')) && safe_attribute?('tracker_id')
593:       if allowed_target_trackers(user).where(:id => t.to_i).exists?
594:         self.tracker_id = t
595:       end
596:     end
597:     if project && tracker.nil?
598:       # Set a default tracker to accept custom field values
599:       # even if tracker is not specified
600:       allowed_trackers = allowed_target_trackers(user)
601: 
602:       if attrs['parent_issue_id'].present?
603:         # If parent_issue_id is present, the first tracker for which this field
604:         # is not disabled is chosen as default
605:         self.tracker = allowed_trackers.detect {|t| t.core_fields.include?('parent_issue_id')}
606:       end
607:       self.tracker ||= allowed_trackers.first
608:     end
609: 
610:     statuses_allowed = new_statuses_allowed_to(user)
611:     if (s = attrs.delete('status_id')) && safe_attribute?('status_id')
612:       if statuses_allowed.collect(&:id).include?(s.to_i)
613:         self.status_id = s
614:       end
615:     end
616:     if new_record? && !statuses_allowed.include?(status)
617:       self.status = statuses_allowed.first || default_status
618:     end
619:     # Use the selected tracker's private default when the form has no explicit value.
620:     if new_record? && tracker&.private_by_default? &&
621:          !attrs.key?('is_private') && safe_attribute?('is_private', user)
622:       attrs['is_private'] = '1'
623:     end
624:     if (u = attrs.delete('assigned_to_id')) && safe_attribute?('assigned_to_id')
625:       self.assigned_to_id = u
626:     end
627:     attrs = delete_unsafe_attributes(attrs, user)
628:     return if attrs.empty?
629: 
630:     if attrs['parent_issue_id'].present?
631:       s = attrs['parent_issue_id'].to_s
632:       unless (m = s.match(%r{\A#?(\d+)\z})) && (m[1] == parent_id.to_s || Issue.visible(user).exists?(m[1]))
633:         @invalid_parent_issue_id = attrs.delete('parent_issue_id')
634:       end
635:     end
636: 
637:     if attrs['custom_field_values'].present?
638:       editable_custom_field_ids = editable_custom_field_values(user).map {|v| v.custom_field_id.to_s}
639:       attrs['custom_field_values'].select! {|k, v| editable_custom_field_ids.include?(k.to_s)}
640:     end
641: 
642:     if attrs['custom_fields'].present?
643:       editable_custom_field_ids = editable_custom_field_values(user).map {|v| v.custom_field_id.to_s}
644:       attrs['custom_fields'].select! {|c| editable_custom_field_ids.include?(c['id'].to_s)}
645:     end
646: 
647:     assign_attributes attrs
648:   end

650:   def disabled_core_fields
651:     tracker ? tracker.disabled_core_fields : []
652:   end

654:   # Returns the custom_field_values that can be edited by the given user
655:   def editable_custom_field_values(user=nil)
656:     read_only = read_only_attribute_names(user)
657:     visible_custom_field_values(user).reject do |value|
658:       read_only.include?(value.custom_field_id.to_s)
659:     end
660:   end
661: 
662:   # Returns the custom fields that can be edited by the given user
663:   def editable_custom_fields(user=nil)
664:     editable_custom_field_values(user).map(&:custom_field).uniq
665:   end
666: 
667:   # Returns the names of attributes that are read-only for user or the current user
668:   # For users with multiple roles, the read-only fields are the intersection of
669:   # read-only fields of each role
670:   # The result is an array of strings where sustom fields are represented with their ids
671:   #
672:   # Examples:
673:   #   issue.read_only_attribute_names # => ['due_date', '2']
674:   #   issue.read_only_attribute_names(user) # => []
675:   def read_only_attribute_names(user=nil)
676:     workflow_rule_by_attribute(user).reject {|attr, rule| rule != 'readonly'}.keys
677:   end
678: 
679:   # Returns the names of required attributes for user or the current user
680:   # For users with multiple roles, the required fields are the intersection of
681:   # required fields of each role
682:   # The result is an array of strings where sustom fields are represented with their ids
683:   #
684:   # Examples:
685:   #   issue.required_attribute_names # => ['due_date', '2']
686:   #   issue.required_attribute_names(user) # => []
687:   def required_attribute_names(user=nil)
688:     workflow_rule_by_attribute(user).reject {|attr, rule| rule != 'required'}.keys
689:   end
690: 
691:   # Returns true if the attribute is required for user
692:   def required_attribute?(name, user=nil)
693:     required_attribute_names(user).include?(name.to_s)
694:   end
695: 
696:   # Returns a hash of the workflow rule by attribute for the given user
697:   #
698:   # Examples:
699:   #   issue.workflow_rule_by_attribute # => {'due_date' => 'required', 'start_date' => 'readonly'}
700:   def workflow_rule_by_attribute(user=nil)
701:     return @workflow_rule_by_attribute if @workflow_rule_by_attribute && user.nil?
702: 
703:     roles = roles_for_workflow(user || User.current)
704:     return {} if roles.empty?
705: 
706:     result = {}
707:     workflow_permissions =
708:       WorkflowPermission.where(
709:         :tracker_id => tracker_id, :old_status_id => status_id,
710:         :role_id => roles.map(&:id)
711:       ).to_a
712:     if workflow_permissions.any?
713:       workflow_rules = workflow_permissions.inject({}) do |h, wp|
714:         h[wp.field_name] ||= {}
715:         h[wp.field_name][wp.role_id] = wp.rule
716:         h
717:       end
718:       fields_with_roles = {}
719:       IssueCustomField.where(:visible => false).
720:         joins(:roles).pluck(:id, "role_id").
721:           each do |field_id, role_id|
722:         fields_with_roles[field_id] ||= []
723:         fields_with_roles[field_id] << role_id
724:       end
725:       roles.each do |role|
726:         fields_with_roles.each do |field_id, role_ids|
727:           unless role_ids.include?(role.id)
728:             field_name = field_id.to_s
729:             workflow_rules[field_name] ||= {}
730:             workflow_rules[field_name][role.id] = 'readonly'
731:           end
732:         end
733:       end
734:       workflow_rules.each do |attr, rules|
735:         next if rules.size < roles.size
736: 
737:         uniq_rules = rules.values.uniq
738:         if uniq_rules.size == 1
739:           result[attr] = uniq_rules.first
740:         else
741:           result[attr] = 'required'
742:         end
743:       end
744:     end
745:     @workflow_rule_by_attribute = result if user.nil?
746:     result
747:   end
748:   private :workflow_rule_by_attribute

752:       status.default_done_ratio
753:     else
754:       read_attribute(:done_ratio)
755:     end
756:   end

766:   def validate_issue
767:     if due_date && start_date && (start_date_changed? || due_date_changed?) && due_date < start_date
768:       errors.add :due_date, :greater_than_start_date
769:     end
770: 
771:     if start_date && start_date_changed? && soonest_start && start_date < soonest_start
772:       errors.add :start_date, :earlier_than_minimum_start_date, :date => format_date(soonest_start)
773:     end
774: 
775:     if project && fixed_version_id
776:       if fixed_version.nil? || assignable_versions.exclude?(fixed_version)
777:         errors.add :fixed_version_id, :inclusion
778:       elsif reopening? && fixed_version.closed?
779:         errors.add :base, I18n.t(:error_can_not_reopen_issue_on_closed_version)
780:       end
781:     end
782: 
783:     if project && category_id
784:       unless project.issue_category_ids.include?(category_id)
785:         errors.add :category_id, :inclusion
786:       end
787:     end
788: 
789:     # Checks that the issue can not be added/moved to a disabled tracker
790:     if project && (tracker_id_changed? || project_id_changed?)
791:       if tracker && !project.trackers.include?(tracker)
792:         errors.add :tracker_id, :inclusion
793:       end
794:     end
795: 
796:     if project && assigned_to_id_changed? && assigned_to_id.present?
797:       unless assignable_users.include?(assigned_to)
798:         errors.add :assigned_to_id, :invalid
799:       end
800:     end
801: 
802:     # Checks parent issue assignment
803:     if @invalid_parent_issue_id.present?
804:       errors.add :parent_issue_id, :invalid
805:     elsif @parent_issue
806:       if !valid_parent_project?(@parent_issue)
807:         errors.add :parent_issue_id, :invalid
808:       elsif (@parent_issue != parent) && (
809:           self.would_reschedule?(@parent_issue) ||
810:           @parent_issue.self_and_ancestors.any? do |a|
811:             a.relations_from.any? do |r|
812:               r.relation_type == IssueRelation::TYPE_PRECEDES &&
813:                  r.issue_to.would_reschedule?(self)
814:             end
815:           end
816:         )
817:         errors.add :parent_issue_id, :invalid
818:       elsif !closed? && @parent_issue.closed?
819:         # cannot attach an open issue to a closed parent
820:         errors.add :base, :open_issue_with_closed_parent
821:       elsif !new_record?
822:         # moving an existing issue
823:         if move_possible?(@parent_issue)
824:           # move accepted
825:         else
826:           errors.add :parent_issue_id, :invalid
827:         end
828:       end
829:     end
830:   end

832:   # Validates the issue against additional workflow requirements
833:   def validate_required_fields
834:     user = new_record? ? author : current_journal.try(:user)
835: 
836:     required_attribute_names(user).each do |attribute|
837:       if /^\d+$/.match?(attribute)
838:         attribute = attribute.to_i
839:         v = custom_field_values.detect {|v| v.custom_field_id == attribute}
840:         if v && Array(v.value).detect(&:present?).nil?
841:           errors.add(v.custom_field.name, l('activerecord.errors.messages.blank'))
842:         end
843:       else
844:         if respond_to?(attribute) && send(attribute).blank? && !disabled_core_fields.include?(attribute)
845:           next if attribute == 'category_id' && project.try(:issue_categories).blank?
846:           next if attribute == 'fixed_version_id' && assignable_versions.blank?
847: 
848:           errors.add attribute, :blank
849:         end
850:       end
851:     end
852:   end

858:       end
859:     end
860:   end

913:   # Returns a scope for journals that have an id greater than journal_id
914:   def journals_after(journal_id)
915:     scope = journals.reorder("#{Journal.table_name}.id ASC")
916:     if journal_id.present?
917:       scope = scope.where("#{Journal.table_name}.id > ?", journal_id.to_i)
918:     end
919:     scope
920:   end

922:   # Returns the journals that are visible to user with their index
923:   # Used to display the issue history
924:   def visible_journals_with_index(user=User.current)
925:     preloads = [:details, :updated_by]
926:     preloads << (Setting.gravatar_enabled? ? {user: :email_address} : :user)
927: 
928:     result = journals.
929:       preload(*preloads).
930:       reorder(:created_on, :id).
931:       to_a
932: 
933:     result.each_with_index {|j, i| j.indice = i + 1}
934: 
935:     unless user.allowed_to?(:view_private_notes, project)
936:       result.select! do |journal|
937:         !journal.private_notes? || journal.user == user
938:       end
939:     end
940:     Journal.preload_journals_details_custom_fields(result)
941:     result.select! {|journal| journal.notes? || journal.visible_details.any?}
942: 
943:     Journal.preload_reaction_details(result)
944: 
945:     result
946:   end

948:   # Returns the assignee immediately prior to the current one from the issue history
949:   def prior_assigned_to
950:     prior_assigned_to_id =
951:       journals.joins(:details)
952:               .where(details: {prop_key: 'assigned_to_id'})
953:               .where.not(details: {old_value: nil})
954:               .order(id: :desc)
955:               .pick(:old_value)
956: 
957:     prior_assigned_to_id && Principal.find_by(id: prior_assigned_to_id)
958:   end

961:   # Returns nil for a new issue
962:   def status_was
963:     if status_id_changed?
964:       if status_id_was.to_i > 0
965:         @status_was ||= IssueStatus.find_by_id(status_id_was)
966:       end
967:     else
968:       @status_was ||= status
969:     end
970:   end

972:   # Return true if the issue is closed, otherwise false
973:   def closed?
974:     status.present? && status.is_closed?
975:   end
976: 
977:   # Returns true if the issue was closed when loaded
978:   def was_closed?
979:     status_was.present? && status_was.is_closed?
980:   end
981: 
982:   # Return true if the issue is being reopened
983:   def reopening?
984:     if new_record?
985:       false
986:     else
987:       status_id_changed? && !closed? && was_closed?
988:     end
989:   end
990:   alias :reopened? :reopening?
991: 
992:   # Return true if the issue is being closed
993:   def closing?
994:     if new_record?
995:       closed?
996:     else
997:       status_id_changed? && closed? && !was_closed?
998:     end
999:   end
1000: 
1001:   # Returns true if the issue is overdue
1002:   def overdue?
1003:     due_date.present? && (due_date < User.current.today) && !closed?
1004:   end
1005: 
1006:   # Is the amount of work done less than it should for the due date
1007:   def behind_schedule?
1008:     return false if start_date.nil? || due_date.nil?
1009: 
1010:     done_date = start_date + ((due_date - start_date + 1) * done_ratio / 100).floor
1011:     return done_date <= User.current.today
1012:   end

1085:   def new_statuses_allowed_to(user=User.current, include_default=false)
1086:     initial_status = nil
1087:     if new_record?
1088:       # nop
1089:     elsif tracker_id_changed?
1090:       if Tracker.where(:id => tracker_id_was, :default_status_id => status_id_was).any?
1091:         initial_status = default_status
1092:       elsif tracker.issue_status_ids.include?(status_id_was)
1093:         initial_status = IssueStatus.find_by_id(status_id_was)
1094:       else
1095:         initial_status = default_status
1096:       end
1097:     else
1098:       initial_status = status_was
1099:     end
1100: 
1101:     initial_assigned_to_id = assigned_to_id_changed? ? assigned_to_id_was : assigned_to_id
1102:     assignee_transitions_allowed = initial_assigned_to_id.present? &&
1103:       (user.id == initial_assigned_to_id || user.group_ids.include?(initial_assigned_to_id))
1104: 
1105:     statuses = []
1106:     statuses += IssueStatus.new_statuses_allowed(
1107:       initial_status,
1108:       roles_for_workflow(user),
1109:       tracker,
1110:       author == user,
1111:       assignee_transitions_allowed
1112:     )
1113:     statuses << initial_status unless statuses.empty?
1114:     statuses << default_status if include_default || (new_record? && statuses.empty?)
1115: 
1116:     statuses = statuses.compact.uniq.sort
1117:     unless closable?
1118:       # cannot close a blocked issue or a parent with open subtasks
1119:       statuses.reject!(&:is_closed?)
1120:     end
1121:     unless reopenable?
1122:       # cannot reopen a subtask of a closed parent
1123:       statuses.select!(&:is_closed?)
1124:     end
1125:     statuses
1126:   end

1338:   # Returns true if this issue blocks the other issue, otherwise returns false
1339:   def blocks?(other)
1340:     all = [self]
1341:     last = [self]
1342:     while last.any?
1343:       current =
1344:         last.map do |i|
1345:           i.relations_from.where(:relation_type => IssueRelation::TYPE_BLOCKS).map(&:issue_to)
1346:         end.flatten.uniq
1347:       current -= last
1348:       current -= all
1349:       return true if current.include?(other)
1350: 
1351:       last = current
1352:       all += last
1353:     end
1354:     false
1355:   end

1357:   # Returns true if the other issue might be rescheduled if the start/due dates of this issue change
1358:   def would_reschedule?(other)
1359:     all = [self]
1360:     last = [self]
1361:     while last.any?
1362:       current = last.map do |i|
1363:         i.relations_from.where(:relation_type => IssueRelation::TYPE_PRECEDES).map(&:issue_to) +
1364:         i.leaves.to_a +
1365:         i.ancestors.map {|a| a.relations_from.where(:relation_type => IssueRelation::TYPE_PRECEDES).map(&:issue_to)}
1366:       end.flatten.uniq
1367:       current -= last
1368:       current -= all
1369:       return true if current.include?(other)
1370: 
1371:       last = current
1372:       all += last
1373:     end
1374:     false
1375:   end

1424:   # Reschedules the issue on the given date or the next working day and saves the record.
1425:   # If the issue is a parent task, this is done by rescheduling its subtasks.
1426:   def reschedule_on!(date, journal=nil)
1427:     return if date.nil?
1428: 
1429:     if leaf? || !dates_derived?
1430:       if start_date.nil? || start_date != date
1431:         if start_date && start_date > date
1432:           # Issue can not be moved earlier than its soonest start date
1433:           date = [soonest_start(true), date].compact.max
1434:         end
1435:         if journal
1436:           init_journal(journal.user)
1437:         end
1438:         reschedule_on(date)
1439:         begin
1440:           save
1441:         rescue ActiveRecord::StaleObjectError
1442:           reload
1443:           reschedule_on(date)
1444:           save
1445:         end
1446:       end
1447:     else
1448:       leaves.each do |leaf|
1449:         if leaf.start_date
1450:           # Only move subtask if it starts at the same date as the parent
1451:           # or if it starts before the given date
1452:           if start_date == leaf.start_date || date > leaf.start_date
1453:             leaf.reschedule_on!(date)
1454:           end
1455:         else
1456:           leaf.reschedule_on!(date)
1457:         end
1458:       end
1459:     end
1460:   end

1628:   # Query generator for selecting groups of issue counts for a project
1629:   # based on specific criteria
1630:   #
1631:   # Options
1632:   # * project - Project to search in.
1633:   # * with_subprojects - Includes subprojects issues if set to true.
1634:   # * association - Symbol. Association for grouping.
1635:   def self.count_and_group_by(options)
1636:     assoc = reflect_on_association(options[:association])
1637:     select_field = assoc.foreign_key
1638: 
1639:     Issue.
1640:       visible(User.current, :project => options[:project], :with_subprojects => options[:with_subprojects]).
1641:       joins(:status).
1642:       group(:status_id, :is_closed, select_field).
1643:       count.
1644:       map do |columns, total|
1645:         status_id, is_closed, field_value = columns
1646:         is_closed = ['t', 'true', '1'].include?(is_closed.to_s)
1647:         {
1648:           "status_id" => status_id.to_s,
1649:           "closed" => is_closed,
1650:           select_field => field_value.to_s,
1651:           "total" => total.to_s
1652:         }
1653:       end
1654:   end

1696:   def self.allowed_target_trackers(project, user=User.current, current_tracker=nil)
1697:     if project
1698:       scope = project.trackers.sorted
1699:       unless user.admin?
1700:         roles = user.roles_for_project(project).select {|r| r.has_permission?(:add_issues)}
1701:         unless roles.any? {|r| r.permissions_all_trackers?(:add_issues)}
1702:           tracker_ids = roles.map {|r| r.permissions_tracker_ids(:add_issues)}.flatten.uniq
1703:           if current_tracker
1704:             tracker_ids << current_tracker
1705:           end
1706:           scope = scope.where(:id => tracker_ids)
1707:         end
1708:       end
1709:       scope
1710:     else
1711:       Tracker.none
1712:     end
1713:   end

1727:       roles.any? do |r|

1734:   def after_project_change
1735:     # Update project_id on related time entries
1736:     TimeEntry.where({:issue_id => id}).update_all(["project_id = ?", project_id])
1737: 
1738:     # Delete issue relations
1739:     unless Setting.cross_project_issue_relations?
1740:       relations_from.clear
1741:       relations_to.clear
1742:     end
1743: 
1744:     # Move subtasks that were in the same project
1745:     children.each do |child|
1746:       next unless child.project_id == project_id_before_last_save
1747: 
1748:       # Change project and keep project
1749:       child.send :project=, project, true
1750:       unless child.save
1751:         errors.add(
1752:           :base,
1753:           l(:error_move_of_child_not_possible,
1754:             :child => "##{child.id}",
1755:             :errors => child.errors.full_messages.join(", "))
1756:         )
1757:         raise ActiveRecord::Rollback
1758:       end
1759:     end
1760:   end

1762:   # Callback for after the creation of an issue by copy
1763:   # * adds a "copied to" relation with the copied issue
1764:   # * copies subtasks from the copied issue
1765:   def after_create_from_copy
1766:     return unless copy? && !@after_create_from_copy_handled
1767: 
1768:     if (@copied_from.project_id == project_id ||
1769:           Setting.cross_project_issue_relations?) &&
1770:         @copy_options[:link] != false
1771:       if @current_journal
1772:         @copied_from.init_journal(@current_journal.user)
1773:       end
1774:       relation =
1775:         IssueRelation.new(:issue_from => @copied_from, :issue_to => self,
1776:                           :relation_type => IssueRelation::TYPE_COPIED_TO)
1777:       unless relation.save
1778:         if logger
1779:           logger.error(
1780:             "Could not create relation while copying ##{@copied_from.id} to ##{id} " \
1781:               "due to validation errors: #{relation.errors.full_messages.join(', ')}"
1782:           )
1783:         end
1784:       end
1785:     end
1786: 
1787:     unless @copied_from.leaf? || @copy_options[:subtasks] == false
1788:       copy_options = (@copy_options || {}).merge(:subtasks => false)
1789:       copied_issue_ids = {@copied_from.id => self.id}
1790:       @copied_from.reload.descendants.reorder("#{Issue.table_name}.lft").each do |child|
1791:         # Do not copy self when copying an issue as a descendant of the copied issue
1792:         next if child == self
1793:         # Do not copy subtasks of issues that were not copied
1794:         next unless copied_issue_ids[child.parent_id]
1795: 
1796:         # Do not copy subtasks that are not visible to avoid potential disclosure of private data
1797:         unless child.visible?
1798:           if logger
1799:             logger.error(
1800:               "Subtask ##{child.id} was not copied during ##{@copied_from.id} copy " \
1801:                 "because it is not visible to the current user"
1802:             )
1803:           end
1804:           next
1805:         end
1806:         copy = Issue.new.copy_from(child, copy_options)
1807:         if @current_journal
1808:           copy.init_journal(@current_journal.user)
1809:         end
1810:         copy.author = author
1811:         copy.project = project
1812:         copy.parent_issue_id = copied_issue_ids[child.parent_id]
1813:         unless child.fixed_version.present? && child.fixed_version.status == 'open'
1814:           copy.fixed_version_id = nil
1815:         end
1816:         unless child.assigned_to_id.present? &&
1817:                  child.assigned_to.status == User::STATUS_ACTIVE
1818:           copy.assigned_to = nil
1819:         end
1820:         unless copy.save
1821:           if logger
1822:             logger.error(
1823:               "Could not copy subtask ##{child.id} " \
1824:                 "while copying ##{@copied_from.id} to ##{id} due to validation errors: " \
1825:                 "#{copy.errors.full_messages.join(', ')}"
1826:             )
1827:           end
1828:           next
1829:         end
1830:         copied_issue_ids[child.id] = copy.id
1831:       end
1832:     end
1833:     @after_create_from_copy_handled = true
1834:   end

1844:   def update_nested_set_attributes_on_parent_change
1845:     former_parent_id = parent_id_before_last_save
1846:     # delete invalid relations of all descendants
1847:     self_and_descendants.each do |issue|
1848:       issue.relations.each do |relation|
1849:         relation.destroy unless relation.valid?
1850:       end
1851:     end
1852:     # update former parent

1863:   def recalculate_attributes_for(issue_id)
1864:     if issue_id && p = Issue.find_by_id(issue_id)
1865:       if p.priority_derived?
1866:         # priority = highest priority of open children
1867:         # priority is left unchanged if all children are closed and there's no default priority defined
1868:         if priority_position =
1869:              p.children.open.joins(:priority).maximum("#{IssuePriority.table_name}.position")
1870:           p.priority = IssuePriority.find_by_position(priority_position)
1871:         elsif default_priority = IssuePriority.default
1872:           p.priority = default_priority
1873:         end
1874:       end
1875: 
1876:       if p.dates_derived?
1877:         # start/due dates = lowest/highest dates of children
1878:         p.start_date = p.children.minimum(:start_date)
1879:         p.due_date = p.children.maximum(:due_date)
1880:         if p.start_date && p.due_date && p.due_date < p.start_date
1881:           p.start_date, p.due_date = p.due_date, p.start_date
1882:         end
1883:       end
1884: 
1885:       if p.done_ratio_derived?
1886:         # done ratio = average ratio of children weighted with their total estimated hours
1887:         unless Issue.use_status_for_done_ratio? && p.status && p.status.default_done_ratio
1888:           children = p.children.to_a
1889:           if children.any?
1890:             child_with_total_estimated_hours = children.select {|c| c.total_estimated_hours.to_f > 0.0}
1891:             if child_with_total_estimated_hours.any?
1892:               average = Rational(
1893:                 child_with_total_estimated_hours.sum(&:total_estimated_hours).to_s,
1894:                 child_with_total_estimated_hours.count
1895:               )
1896:             else
1897:               average = Rational(1)
1898:             end
1899:             done = children.sum do |c|
1900:               estimated = Rational(c.total_estimated_hours.to_f.to_s)
1901:               estimated = average unless estimated > 0.0
1902:               ratio = c.closed? ? 100 : (c.done_ratio || 0)
1903:               estimated * ratio
1904:             end
1905:             progress = Rational(done, average * children.count)
1906:             p.done_ratio = progress.floor
1907:           end
1908:         end
1909:       end
1910: 
1911:       # ancestors will be recursively updated
1912:       p.save(:validate => false)
1913:     end
1914:   end

1921:       # Only need to update issues with a fixed_version from

1923:       Issue.joins(:project, :fixed_version).
1924:         where("#{Issue.table_name}.fixed_version_id IS NOT NULL" +
1925:           " AND #{Issue.table_name}.project_id <> #{::Version.table_name}.project_id" +
1926:           " AND #{::Version.table_name}.sharing <> 'system'").
1927:         where(conditions).each do |issue|
1928:         next if issue.project.nil? || issue.fixed_version.nil?
1929: 
1930:         unless issue.project.shared_versions.include?(issue.fixed_version)
1931:           retried = false
1932:           begin
1933:             issue.init_journal(User.current)
1934:             issue.fixed_version = nil
1935:             issue.save

1937:             raise if retried

20: module Redmine
21:   module Acts
22:     module Customizable
23:       def self.included(base)
24:         base.extend ClassMethods
25:       end
26: 
27:       module ClassMethods
28:         def acts_as_customizable(options = {})
29:           return if self.included_modules.include?(Redmine::Acts::Customizable::InstanceMethods)
30:           cattr_accessor :customizable_options
31:           self.customizable_options = options
32:           before_destroy :store_attachment_custom_value_ids
33:           has_many :custom_values, lambda {includes(:custom_field)},
34:                                    :as => :customized,
35:                                    :inverse_of => :customized,
36:                                    :dependent => :delete_all,
37:                                    :validate => false
38: 
39:           send :include, Redmine::Acts::Customizable::InstanceMethods
40:           validate :validate_custom_field_values
41:           after_save :save_custom_field_values
42:           after_destroy :destroy_custom_value_attachments
43:         end
44:       end
45: 
46:       module InstanceMethods
47:         def self.included(base)
48:           base.extend ClassMethods
49:         end
50: 
51:         def available_custom_fields
52:           CustomField.where("type = '#{self.class.name}CustomField'").sorted.to_a
53:         end
54: 
55:         # Sets the values of the object's custom fields
56:         # values is an array like [{'id' => 1, 'value' => 'foo'}, {'id' => 2, 'value' => 'bar'}]
57:         def custom_fields=(values)
58:           values_to_hash = values.inject({}) do |hash, v|
59:             v = v.stringify_keys
60:             if v['id'] && v.has_key?('value')
61:               hash[v['id']] = v['value']
62:             end
63:             hash
64:           end
65:           self.custom_field_values = values_to_hash
66:         end
67: 
68:         # Sets the values of the object's custom fields
69:         # values is a hash like {'1' => 'foo', 2 => 'bar'}
70:         def custom_field_values=(values)
71:           values = values.stringify_keys
72: 
73:           custom_field_values.each do |custom_field_value|
74:             key = custom_field_value.custom_field_id.to_s
75:             if values.has_key?(key)
76:               custom_field_value.value = values[key]
77:             end
78:           end
79:           @custom_field_values_changed = true
80:         end
81: 
82:         def custom_field_values
83:           @custom_field_values ||= available_custom_fields.collect do |field|
84:             x = CustomFieldValue.new
85:             x.custom_field = field
86:             x.customized = self
87:             if field.multiple?
88:               values = custom_values.select { |v| v.custom_field == field }
89:               if values.empty?
90:                 values << custom_values.build(:customized => self, :custom_field => field)
91:               end
92:               x.instance_variable_set("@value", values.map(&:value))
93:             else
94:               cv = custom_values.detect { |v| v.custom_field == field }
95:               cv ||= custom_values.build(:customized => self, :custom_field => field)
96:               x.instance_variable_set("@value", cv.value)
97:             end
98:             x.value_was = x.value.dup if x.value
99:             x
100:           end
101:         end
102: 
103:         def visible_custom_field_values
104:           custom_field_values.select(&:visible?)
105:         end
106: 
107:         def custom_field_values_changed?
108:           @custom_field_values_changed == true
109:         end
110: 
111:         # Should the default custom field value be set for the given custom_value?
112:         # By default, default custom field value is set for new objects only
113:         def set_custom_field_default?(custom_value)
114:           new_record?
115:         end
116: 
117:         def custom_value_for(c)
118:           field_id = (c.is_a?(CustomField) ? c.id : c.to_i)
119:           custom_values.detect {|v| v.custom_field_id == field_id }
120:         end

20: # ActsAsWatchable
21: module Redmine
22:   module Acts
23:     module Watchable
24:       def self.included(base)
25:         base.extend ClassMethods
26:       end
27: 
28:       module ClassMethods
29:         def acts_as_watchable(options = {})
30:           return if self.included_modules.include?(Redmine::Acts::Watchable::InstanceMethods)
31: 
32:           class_eval do
33:             has_many :watchers, :as => :watchable, :dependent => :delete_all
34:             has_many :watcher_users, :through => :watchers, :source => :user, :validate => false
35: 
36:             scope :watched_by, lambda { |principal|
37:               user_ids = Array(principal.id)
38:               user_ids |= principal.group_ids if principal.is_a?(User)
39:               user_ids.compact!
40: 
41:               joins(:watchers).
42:               where("#{Watcher.table_name}.user_id IN (?)", user_ids)
43:             }
44:           end
45:           send :include, Redmine::Acts::Watchable::InstanceMethods
46:         end
47:       end
48: 
49:       module InstanceMethods
50:         def self.included(base)
51:           base.extend ClassMethods
52:         end
53: 
54:         # array of watchers that the given user is allowed to see
55:         def visible_watcher_users(user = User.current)
56:           if user.allowed_to?(:"view_#{self.class.name.underscore}_watchers", project)
57:             watcher_users
58:           else
59:             # without permission, the user can only see themselves (if they're a watcher)
60:             watcher_users & [user]
61:           end
62:         end
63: 
64:         # true if user can be added as a watcher
65:         def valid_watcher?(user)
66:           return true unless respond_to?(:visible?)
67:           return true unless user.is_a?(User)
68: 
69:           visible?(user)
70:         end
71: 
72:         # Adds user as a watcher
73:         def add_watcher(user)
74:           if persisted?
75:             # Rails does not reset the has_many :through association
76:             watcher_users.reset
77:             self.watchers << Watcher.new(:user => user)
78:           else
79:             self.watcher_users << user
80:           end
81:         end
82: 
83:         # Removes user from the watchers list
84:         def remove_watcher(user)
85:           return nil unless user && (user.is_a?(User) || user.is_a?(Group))
86: 
87:           if persisted?
88:             # Rails does not reset the has_many :through association
89:             watcher_users.reset
90:             watchers.where(:user_id => user.id).delete_all
91:           else
92:             watcher_users.delete(user)
93:           end
94:         end
95: 
96:         # Adds/removes watcher
97:         def set_watcher(user, watching=true)
98:           watching ? add_watcher(user) : remove_watcher(user)
99:         end
100: 
101:         # Overrides watcher_user_ids= to make user_ids uniq
102:         def watcher_user_ids=(user_ids)
103:           if user_ids.is_a?(Array)
104:             user_ids = user_ids.uniq
105:           end
106:           super user_ids
107:         end
108: 
109:         # Returns true if object is watched by +principal+, that is
110:         # either by a given group,
111:         # or by a given user or any of their groups
112:         def watched_by?(principal)
113:           return false unless principal
114: 
115:           user_ids = Array(principal.id)
116:           user_ids |= principal.group_ids if principal.is_a?(User)
117:           user_ids.compact!
118: 
119:           (self.watcher_user_ids & user_ids).any?
120:         end

22: module Redmine

29:       module ClassMethods
30:         # Adds the search methods to the class.
31:         #
32:         # Options:
33:         # * :columns - a column or an array of columns to search
34:         # * :project_key - project foreign key (default to project_id)
35:         # * :date_column - name of the datetime column used to sort results (default to :created_on)
36:         # * :permission - permission required to search the model
37:         # * :scope - scope used to search results
38:         # * :preload - associations to preload when loading results for display
39:         def acts_as_searchable(options = {})
40:           return if self.included_modules.include?(Redmine::Acts::Searchable::InstanceMethods)
41:           options.assert_valid_keys(:columns, :project_key, :date_column, :permission, :scope, :preload)
42: 
43:           cattr_accessor :searchable_options
44:           self.searchable_options = options
45: 
46:           if searchable_options[:columns].nil?
47:             raise 'No searchable column defined.'
48:           elsif !searchable_options[:columns].is_a?(Array)
49:             searchable_options[:columns] = [] << searchable_options[:columns]
50:           end
51: 
52:           searchable_options[:project_key] ||= "#{table_name}.project_id"
53:           searchable_options[:date_column] ||= :created_on
54: 
55:           # Should we search additional associations on this model ?
56:           searchable_options[:search_custom_fields] = reflect_on_association(:custom_values).present?
57:           searchable_options[:search_attachments] = reflect_on_association(:attachments).present?
58:           searchable_options[:search_journals] = reflect_on_association(:journals).present?
59: 
60:           send :include, Redmine::Acts::Searchable::InstanceMethods
61:         end
62:       end
63: 
64:       module InstanceMethods
65:         def self.included(base)
66:           base.extend ClassMethods
67:         end
68: 
69:         module ClassMethods
70:           # Searches the model for the given tokens and user visibility.
71:           # The projects argument can be either nil (will search all projects), a project or an array of projects.
72:           # Returns an array that contains the rank and id of all results.
73:           # In current implementation, the rank is the record timestamp converted as an integer.
74:           #
75:           # Valid options:
76:           # * :titles_only - searches tokens in the first searchable column only
77:           # * :all_words - searches results that match all token
78:           # * :
79:           # * :limit - maximum number of results to return
80:           #
81:           # Example:
82:           #   Issue.search_result_ranks_and_ids("foo")
83:           #   # => [[1419595329, 69], [1419595622, 123]]
84:           def search_result_ranks_and_ids(tokens, user=User.current, projects=nil, options={})
85:             tokens = [] << tokens unless tokens.is_a?(Array)
86:             projects = [] << projects if projects.is_a?(Project)
87: 
88:             columns = searchable_options[:columns]
89:             columns = columns[0..0] if options[:titles_only]
90: 
91:             r = []
92:             queries = 0
93: 
94:             unless options[:attachments] == 'only'
95:               r = fetch_ranks_and_ids(
96:                 search_scope(user, projects, options).
97:                 where(search_tokens_condition(columns, tokens, options[:all_words])),
98:                 options[:limit]
99:               )
100:               queries += 1
101:               if !options[:titles_only] && searchable_options[:search_custom_fields]
102:                 searchable_custom_fields = CustomField.where(:type => "#{self.name}CustomField", :searchable => true).to_a
103:                 if searchable_custom_fields.any?
104:                   fields_by_visibility = searchable_custom_fields.group_by {|field|
105:                     field.visibility_by_project_condition(searchable_options[:project_key], user, "#{CustomValue.table_name}.custom_field_id")
106:                   }
107:                   clauses = []
108:                   fields_by_visibility.each do |visibility, fields|
109:                     clauses << "(#{CustomValue.table_name}.custom_field_id IN (#{fields.map(&:id).join(',')}) AND (#{visibility}))"
110:                   end
111:                   visibility = clauses.join(' OR ')
112:                   scope =
113:                     search_scope(user, projects, options)
114:                     .joins(:custom_values)
115:                     .where(visibility)
116:                     .where(search_tokens_condition(["#{CustomValue.table_name}.value"], tokens, options[:all_words]))
117: 
118:                   if self == Issue && Redmine::Database.mysql? && ActiveRecord::Base.connection.supports_optimizer_hints?
119:                     # Force the join order to evaluate issues before custom_values.
120:                     # This avoids inefficient query plans where custom_values are scanned

31:                               :title => :title,
32:                               :description => :description,
33:                               :author => :author,
34:                               :url => {:controller => 'welcome'},
35:                               :type => self.name.underscore.dasherize }
36: 
37:           cattr_accessor :event_options
38:           self.event_options = default_options.merge(options)
39:           send :include, Redmine::Acts::Event::InstanceMethods
40:         end
41:       end
42: 
43:       module InstanceMethods
44:         def self.included(base)
45:           base.extend ClassMethods
46:         end
47: 
48:         %w(datetime title description author type).each do |attr|
49:           src = <<-END_SRC
50:             def event_#{attr}
51:               option = event_options[:#{attr}]
52:               if option.is_a?(Proc)
53:                 option.call(self)
54:               elsif option.is_a?(Symbol)
55:                 send(option)
56:               else
57:                 option
58:               end
59:             end
60:           END_SRC
61:           class_eval src, __FILE__, __LINE__
62:         end
63: 
64:         def event_date
65:           event_datetime.to_date
66:         end
67: 
68:         def event_group
69:           group = event_options[:group] ? send(event_options[:group]) : self
70:           group || self
71:         end
72: 
73:         def event_url(options = {})
74:           option = event_options[:url]
75:           if option.is_a?(Proc)
76:             option.call(self).merge(options)
77:           elsif option.is_a?(Hash)
78:             option.merge(options)
79:           elsif option.is_a?(Symbol)
80:             send(option).merge(options)
81:           else
82:             option
83:           end
84:         end
85: 
86:         # Returns the mail addresses of users that should be notified
87:         def recipients
88:           notified = project.notified_users
89:           notified.reject! {|user| !visible?(user)}
90:           notified.collect(&:mail)
91:         end
92: 
93:         module ClassMethods
94:         end
95:       end

20: module Redmine
21:   module Acts
22:     module ActivityProvider
23:       def self.included(base)
24:         base.extend ClassMethods
25:       end
26: 
27:       module ClassMethods
28:         def acts_as_activity_provider(options = {})
29:           unless self.included_modules.include?(Redmine::Acts::ActivityProvider::InstanceMethods)
30:             cattr_accessor :activity_provider_options
31:             send :include, Redmine::Acts::ActivityProvider::InstanceMethods
32:           end
33: 
34:           options.assert_valid_keys(:type, :permission, :timestamp, :author_key, :scope)
35:           self.activity_provider_options ||= {}
36: 
37:           # One model can provide different event types
38:           # We store these options in activity_provider_options hash
39:           event_type = options.delete(:type) || self.name.underscore.pluralize
40: 
41:           options[:timestamp] ||= "#{table_name}.created_on"
42:           options[:author_key] = "#{table_name}.#{options[:author_key]}" if options[:author_key].is_a?(Symbol)
43:           self.activity_provider_options[event_type] = options
44:         end
45:       end
46: 
47:       module InstanceMethods
48:         def self.included(base)
49:           base.extend ClassMethods
50:         end
51: 
52:         module ClassMethods
53:           # Returns events of type event_type visible by user that occurred between from and to
54:           def find_events(event_type, user, from, to, options)
55:             provider_options = activity_provider_options[event_type]
56:             raise "#{self.name} can not provide #{event_type} events." if provider_options.nil?
57: 
58:             scope = provider_options[:scope]
59:             if !scope
60:               scope = self
61:             elsif scope.respond_to?(:call)
62:               scope = scope.call
63:             else
64:               Rails.application.deprecators[:redmine].warn "acts_as_activity_provider with implicit :scope option is deprecated. Please pass a scope on the #{self.name} as a proc."
65:             end
66: 
67:             scope = scope.where("#{provider_options[:timestamp]} >= ?", from) if from
68:             scope = scope.where("#{provider_options[:timestamp]} <= ?", to) if to
69: 
70:             if options[:author]
71:               return [] if provider_options[:author_key].nil?
72:               scope = scope.where("#{provider_options[:author_key]} = ?", options[:author].id)
73:             end
74: 
75:             if options[:limit]
76:               # id and creation time should be in same order in most cases
77:               scope = scope.reorder("#{table_name}.id DESC").limit(options[:limit])
78:             end
79: 
80:             if provider_options.has_key?(:permission)
81:               scope = scope.where(Project.allowed_to_condition(user, provider_options[:permission] || :view_project, options))
82:             elsif respond_to?(:visible)
83:               scope = scope.visible(user, options)
84:             else
85:               Rails.application.deprecators[:redmine].warn "acts_as_activity_provider with implicit :permission option is deprecated. Add a visible scope to the #{self.name} model or use explicit :permission option."
86:               scope = scope.where(Project.allowed_to_condition(user, "view_#{self.name.underscore.pluralize}".to_sym, options))
87:             end
88: 
89:             if options[:last_by_project]
90:               scope = scope.group("#{Project.table_name}.id").maximum(provider_options[:timestamp])
91:             end
92: 
93:             scope.to_a
94:           end
95:         end
96:       end
97:     end
98:   end
99: end

28:         def acts_as_webhookable(events = %w(created updated deleted))

30:           WebhookPayload.register_model(self, events)
31: 
32:           events.each do |event|
33:             case event
34:             when 'created'
35:               after_create_commit ->{ Webhook.trigger(event_name('created'), self) }
36:             when 'updated'

38:             when 'deleted'
39:               after_destroy_commit ->{ Webhook.trigger(event_name('deleted'), self) }
40:             end

44:         end

50:         end

54:             type: event_name(action),
55:             timestamp: webhook_payload_timestamp(action),
56:             data: {
57:               self.class.model_name.singular.to_sym =>

20: module Redmine
21:   module NestedSet
22:     module IssueNestedSet
23:       def self.included(base)
24:         base.class_eval do
25:           belongs_to :parent, :class_name => self.name
26: 
27:           before_create :add_to_nested_set, :if => lambda {|issue| issue.parent.present?}
28:           after_create :add_as_root, :if => lambda {|issue| issue.parent.blank?}
29:           before_update :handle_parent_change, :if => lambda {|issue| issue.parent_id_changed?}
30:           before_destroy :destroy_children
31:         end
32:         base.extend ClassMethods
33:         base.send :include, Redmine::NestedSet::Traversing
34:       end
35: 
36:       private
37: 
38:       def target_lft
39:         scope_for_max_rgt = self.class.where(:root_id => root_id).where(:parent_id => parent_id)
40:         if id
41:           scope_for_max_rgt = scope_for_max_rgt.where(id: ...id)
42:         end
43:         max_rgt = scope_for_max_rgt.maximum(:rgt)
44:         if max_rgt
45:           max_rgt + 1
46:         elsif parent
47:           parent.lft + 1
48:         else
49:           1
50:         end
51:       end
52: 
53:       def add_to_nested_set(lock=true)
54:         if lock
55:           lock_nested_set { add_to_nested_set_without_lock }
56:         else
57:           add_to_nested_set_without_lock
58:         end
59:       end
60: 
61:       def add_to_nested_set_without_lock
62:         parent.send :reload_nested_set_values
63:         self.root_id = parent.root_id
64:         self.lft = target_lft
65:         self.rgt = lft + 1
66:         self.class.where(:root_id => root_id).where("lft >= ? OR rgt >= ?", lft, lft).update_all(
67:           [
68:             "lft = CASE WHEN lft >= :lft THEN lft + 2 ELSE lft END, " +
69:               "rgt = CASE WHEN rgt >= :lft THEN rgt + 2 ELSE rgt END",
70:             {:lft => lft}
71:           ]
72:         )
73:       end
74: 
75:       def add_as_root
76:         self.root_id = id
77:         self.lft = 1
78:         self.rgt = 2
79:         self.class.where(:id => id).update_all(:root_id => root_id, :lft => lft, :rgt => rgt)
80:       end
81: 
82:       def handle_parent_change
83:         lock_nested_set do
84:           reload_nested_set_values
85:           if parent_id_was
86:             remove_from_nested_set
87:           end
88:           if parent
89:             move_to_nested_set
90:           end
91:           reload_nested_set_values
92:         end
93:       end
94: 
95:       def move_to_nested_set
96:         if parent
97:           previous_root_id = root_id
98:           self.root_id = parent.root_id
99: 
100:           lft_after_move = target_lft
101:           self.class.where(:root_id => parent.root_id).update_all(
102:             [
103:               "lft = CASE WHEN lft >= :lft THEN lft + :shift ELSE lft END, " +
104:                 "rgt = CASE WHEN rgt >= :lft THEN rgt + :shift ELSE rgt END",
105:               {:lft => lft_after_move, :shift => (rgt - lft + 1)}
106:             ]
107:           )
108:           self.class.where(:root_id => previous_root_id).update_all(
109:             [
110:               "root_id = :root_id, lft = lft + :shift, rgt = rgt + :shift",
111:               {:root_id => parent.root_id, :shift => lft_after_move - lft}
112:             ]
113:           )
114:           self.lft, self.rgt = lft_after_move, (rgt - lft + lft_after_move)
115:           parent.send :reload_nested_set_values
116:         end
117:       end
118: 
119:       def remove_from_nested_set
120:         self.class.where(:root_id => root_id).where("lft >= ? AND rgt <= ?", lft, rgt).

20: module Redmine
21:   module Reaction
22:     # Types of objects that can have reactions
23:     REACTABLE_TYPES = %w(Journal Issue Message News Comment)
24: 
25:     # Returns true if the user can view the reaction of the object
26:     def self.visible?(object, user = User.current)
27:       Setting.reactions_enabled? && object.visible?(user)
28:     end
29: 
30:     # Returns true if the user can add/remove a reaction to/from the object
31:     def self.editable?(object, user = User.current)
32:       user.logged? && visible?(object, user) && object&.project&.active?
33:     end
34: 
35:     module Reactable
36:       extend ActiveSupport::Concern
37: 
38:       included do
39:         has_many :reactions, as: :reactable, dependent: :delete_all
40: 
41:         attr_writer :reaction_detail
42:       end
43: 
44:       class_methods do
45:         # Preloads reaction details for a collection of objects
46:         def preload_reaction_details(objects)
47:           return unless Setting.reactions_enabled?
48: 
49:           details = ::Reaction.build_detail_map_for(objects, User.current)
50: 
51:           objects.each do |object|
52:             object.reaction_detail = details.fetch(object.id) { ::Reaction::Detail.new }
53:           end
54:         end
55:       end
56: 
57:       def reaction_detail
58:         # Loads and returns reaction details if they are not already loaded.
59:         # This is intended for cases where explicit preloading is unnecessary,
60:         # such as retrieving reactions for a single issue on its detail page.
61:         load_reaction_detail unless defined?(@reaction_detail)
62:         @reaction_detail
63:       end
64: 
65:       def load_reaction_detail
66:         self.class.preload_reaction_details([self])
67:       end
68:     end
69:   end
70: end

20: module Issue::Webhookable
21:   extend ActiveSupport::Concern
22: 
23:   def webhook_payload(user, action)
24:     h = super
25:     if action == 'updated' && current_journal.present?
26:       journal = journals.visible(user).find_by_id(current_journal.id)
27:       if journal.present?
28:         h[:data][:journal] = journal_payload(journal, user)
29:         h[:timestamp] = journal.created_on.iso8601
30:       end
31:     end
32:     h
33:   end
34: 
35:   private
36: 
37:   def journal_payload(journal, user)
38:     {
39:       id: journal.id,
40:       created_on: journal.created_on.iso8601,
41:       notes: journal.notes,
42:       user: {
43:         id: journal.user.id,
44:         name: journal.user.name,
45:       },
46:       details: journal.visible_details(user).map do |d|
47:         {
48:           property: d.property,
49:           prop_key: d.prop_key,
50:           old_value: d.old_value,
51:           value: d.value,
52:         }
53:       end
54:     }
55:   end
56: end

351:     @copy = params[:copy].present?
352: 
353:     attributes = parse_params_for_bulk_update(params[:issue])
354:     copy_subtasks = (params[:copy_subtasks] == '1')
355:     copy_watchers = (params[:copy_watchers] == '1')
356: 
357:     if @copy
358:       unless User.current.allowed_to?(:copy_issues, @projects)
359:         raise ::Unauthorized
360:       end
361: 
362:       target_projects = @projects
363:       if attributes['project_id'].present?
364:         target_projects = Project.where(:id => attributes['project_id']).to_a
365:       end
366:       unless User.current.allowed_to?(:add_issues, target_projects)
367:         raise ::Unauthorized
368:       end
369: 
370:       unless User.current.allowed_to?(:add_issue_watchers, @projects)
371:         copy_watchers = false
372:       end
373:     else
374:       unless @issues.all?(&:attributes_editable?)
375:         raise ::Unauthorized
376:       end
377:     end
378: 
379:     unsaved_issues = []
380:     saved_issues = []
381: 
382:     if @copy && copy_subtasks
383:       # Descendant issues will be copied with the parent task
384:       # Don't copy them twice
385:       @issues.reject! {|issue| @issues.detect {|other| issue.is_descendant_of?(other)}}
386:     end
387: 
388:     @issues.each do |orig_issue|
389:       orig_issue.reload
390:       if @copy
391:         issue = orig_issue.copy(
392:           {},
393:           :attachments => copy_attachments?(params[:copy_attachments]),
394:           :subtasks => copy_subtasks,
395:           :watchers => copy_watchers,
396:           :link => link_copy?(params[:link_copy])
397:         )
398:       else
399:         issue = orig_issue
400:       end

403:       call_hook(:controller_issues_bulk_edit_before_save, {:params => params, :issue => issue})
404:       if issue.save
405:         saved_issues << issue
406:       else
407:         unsaved_issues << orig_issue
408:       end
409:     end
410: 
411:     if unsaved_issues.empty?
412:       flash[:notice] = l(:notice_successful_update) unless saved_issues.empty?
413:       if params[:follow]
414:         if @issues.size == 1 && saved_issues.size == 1

417:           redirect_to project_issues_path(saved_issues.map(&:project).first)
418:         end
419:       else
420:         redirect_back_or_default _project_issues_path(@project)
421:       end

423:       @saved_issues = @issues

425:       @issues = Issue.visible.where(:id => @unsaved_issues.map(&:id)).to_a

442:   def replace_none_values_with_blank(params)
443:     attributes = (params || {})
444:     attributes.each_key {|k| attributes[k] = '' if attributes[k] == 'none'}
445:     if (custom = attributes[:custom_field_values])

1: # frozen_string_literal: true
2:

18: # Foundation, Inc., 51 Franklin Street, Fifth Floor, Boston, MA  02110-1301, USA.
19: 
20: class Journal < ApplicationRecord
21:   include Redmine::SafeAttributes
22:   include Redmine::Reaction::Reactable
23: 
24:   belongs_to :journalized, :polymorphic => true
25:   # added as a quick fix to allow eager loading of the polymorphic association
26:   # since always associated to an issue, for now
27:   belongs_to :issue, :foreign_key => :journalized_id
28: 
29:   belongs_to :user
30:   belongs_to :updated_by, :class_name => 'User'
31:   has_many :details, :class_name => "JournalDetail", :dependent => :delete_all, :inverse_of => :journal
32:   attr_accessor :indice
33: 
34:   acts_as_event(
35:     :title =>
36:        Proc.new do |o|
37:          status = ((s = o.new_status) ? " (#{s})" : nil)
38:          "#{o.issue.tracker} ##{o.issue.id}#{status}: #{o.issue.subject}"
39:        end,
40:     :description => :notes,
41:     :author => :user,
42:     :group => :issue,
43:     :type =>
44:       Proc.new do |o|
45:         (s = o.new_status) ? (s.is_closed? ? 'issue-closed' : 'issue-edit') : 'issue-note'
46:       end,
47:     :url =>
48:       Proc.new do |o|
49:         {:controller => 'issues', :action => 'show', :id => o.issue.id, :anchor => "change-#{o.id}"}
50:       end
51:   )
52:   acts_as_activity_provider(
53:     :type => 'issues',
54:     :author_key => :user_id,
55:     :scope =>
56:       proc do
57:         preload({:issue => :project}, {:issue => :tracker}, :user).
58:           joins("LEFT OUTER JOIN #{JournalDetail.table_name} ON #{JournalDetail.table_name}.journal_id = #{Journal.table_name}.id").
59:             where("#{Journal.table_name}.journalized_type = 'Issue' AND" +
60:                   " (#{JournalDetail.table_name}.prop_key = 'status_id' OR #{Journal.table_name}.notes <> '')").distinct
61:       end
62:   )
63:   acts_as_mentionable :attributes => ['notes']
64:   before_create :split_private_notes
65:   before_create :add_watcher
66:   after_create_commit :send_notification
67: 
68:   scope :visible, (lambda do |*args|
69:     user = args.shift || User.current
70:     options = args.shift || {}
71: 
72:     joins(:issue => :project).
73:       where(Issue.visible_condition(user, options)).
74:       where(Journal.visible_notes_condition(user, :skip_pre_condition => true))
75:   end)
76: 
77:   safe_attributes(
78:     'notes',
79:     :if => lambda {|journal, user| journal.new_record? || journal.editable_by?(user)})
80:   safe_attributes(
81:     'private_notes',
82:     :if => lambda {|journal, user| user.allowed_to?(:set_notes_private, journal.project)})
83:   safe_attributes 'updated_by'
84: 
85:   # Returns a SQL condition to filter out journals with notes that are not visible to user
86:   def self.visible_notes_condition(user=User.current, options={})
87:     private_notes_permission = Project.allowed_to_condition(user, :view_private_notes, options)
88:     sanitize_sql_for_conditions(["(#{table_name}.private_notes = ? OR #{table_name}.user_id = ? OR (#{private_notes_permission}))", false, user.id])
89:   end
90: 
91:   def initialize(*args)
92:     super
93:     if journalized
94:       if journalized.new_record?
95:         self.notify = false
96:       else
97:         start
98:       end
99:     end
100:   end
101: 
102:   def save(*args)
103:     journalize_changes
104:     # Do not save an empty journal
105:     notes_and_details_empty? ? false : super()

21:   def perform(hook_id, payload_json)
22:     if hook = Webhook.find_by_id(hook_id)
23:       if hook.user&.active?
24:         User.current = hook.user
25:         hook.call payload_json
26:       else
27:         Rails.logger.debug { "WebhookJob: user with id=#{hook.user_id} is not active" }
28:       end
29:     else
30:       Rails.logger.debug { "WebhookJob: couldn't find hook with id=#{hook_id}" }
31:     end
32:   end
